subtopics.subtopic_description: Educational background or training
notes: Holds a Master’s Degree
Completed graduate studies in nonprofit management, public administration, or a related field
Attended fundraising and grant writing workshops throughout career
Participated in ongoing professional development seminars for fundraising professionals

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific job title and role description
notes: Current job title: Senior Fundraising Manager
Responsible for developing and implementing fundraising strategies
Oversees donor relations, grant proposals, and campaign planning
Acts as a liaison between donors and program teams

subtopics.subtopic_description: Current industry or sector (e.g., tech, finance, manufacturing)
notes: Works in the nonprofit sector
Focuses on social impact and charitable organizations
Experience includes education, health, and/or human services causes

subtopics.subtopic_description: Company size and environment
notes: Organization employs approximately 50-150 staff members
Works in a collaborative, mission-driven environment
Small to medium-sized nonprofit with a close-knit team culture

subtopics.subtopic_description: Type of business or market segment
notes: Organization operates as a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit
Primary funding comes from individual donors, grants, and corporate partnerships
Focuses on community engagement and social good initiatives

subtopics.subtopic_description: Duration/years of experience in current role
notes: Has over 10 years of experience in fundraising roles
Served in a senior fundraising capacity for at least 7 years
Held prior related roles in donor relations and development

subtopics.subtopic_description: Professional seniority or career level
notes: Occupies a senior-level management position
Acts as a mentor to junior fundraising staff
Frequently involved in strategic decision-making and planning

subtopics.subtopic_description: Primary job responsibilities and regular daily tasks
notes: Builds and maintains relationships with donors and sponsors
Writes and edits grant proposals and funding applications
Plans and executes fundraising campaigns and events
Manages donor database and tracks contributions
Collaborates with program teams to communicate impact

subtopics.subtopic_description: Approximate proportion of time spent on core activities
notes: Spends approximately 40% of time on donor outreach and stewardship
Devotes 25% of work week to grant writing and research
Allocates 15% to campaign/event planning and execution
Uses 10% for team meetings and internal collaboration
Reserves 10% for administrative and reporting tasks

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of autonomy and scope of decision-making in the role
notes: Has significant autonomy in strategizing and prioritizing fundraising activities
Authorized to make decisions regarding outreach methods and communication style
Consults with executive leadership for major campaign direction and budget approvals
Empowered to negotiate donor agreements within set guidelines

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that feel easiest or most natural to perform
notes: Engaging in one-on-one conversations with long-term donors
Drafting personalized thank-you notes and stewardship updates
Sharing impact stories and organizational successes
Identifying donor interests from CRM data

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks perceived as most challenging or complex
notes: Coordinating large-scale fundraising events with multiple stakeholders
Writing highly competitive grant proposals with strict criteria
Managing major donor relationships with complex expectations
Analyzing large datasets to identify giving patterns and trends

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that are repetitive, data-heavy, or suitable for automation
notes: Updating donor records and contact details in CRM systems
Generating routine fundraising reports and donation summaries
Researching preliminary grant opportunities
Segmenting email lists for targeted communications

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that are most enjoyable or engaging versus those that feel boring or tedious
notes: Finds donor relationship building and storytelling highly rewarding
Enjoys strategic planning sessions with the fundraising team
Considers manual data entry and list-cleaning tedious
Prefers interactive tasks over repetitive administrative work

subtopics.subtopic_description: Common pain points or inefficiencies in completing tasks
notes: Manual data entry and cleanup in CRM can be time-consuming
Difficulty in integrating data across multiple platforms
Grant research is often repetitive and labor-intensive
Preparing individualized reports for major donors can be resource-intensive

subtopics.subtopic_description: How enjoyment, skill level, and productivity relate to one another
notes: Productivity is highest when working on relationship-driven tasks
Enjoyment and proficiency are closely linked in donor communication activities
Less skilled or tedious tasks, such as data cleanup, tend to reduce engagement
Prefers to automate or delegate repetitive tasks to focus on high-impact activities

subtopics.subtopic_description: Attitude towards learning new technologies and tools
notes: Open and proactive in exploring new technologies for fundraising
Views tech adoption as necessary for staying effective and competitive
Embraces digital transformation in the nonprofit sector

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived adaptability to new software/methods
notes: Adapts quickly to new CRM features and updates
Comfortable experimenting with new digital marketing platforms
Regularly seeks out webinars and tutorials for the latest tools

subtopics.subtopic_description: Willingness to invest time in tech training
notes: Allocates time monthly for tech skill development
Willing to invest in formal training if it improves fundraising results
Encourages team to participate in ongoing technology training

subtopics.subtopic_description: Motivations or barriers to learning new tech (e.g., workload, relevance)
notes: Motivated by potential to save time and improve donor engagement
Main barrier is balancing tech learning with high workload periods
Adopts new tools if they directly support fundraising goals

subtopics.subtopic_description: Influence of peers or management on willingness to adopt new tools
notes: Peers’ positive experiences with tech influence adoption decisions
Management support for digital innovation encourages experimentation
Participates in team discussions before implementing new tools

topic: Primary Tools and Technologies Used in Work

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific software, platforms, or systems used daily
notes: Uses Salesforce or Raiser’s Edge CRM daily to manage donor data
Relies on email marketing tools like Mailchimp weekly for communications
Utilizes Google Workspace for collaboration and document sharing
Leverages LLM-based tools for drafting communications and reports

subtopics.subtopic_description: Essential non-AI tools for workflow
notes: Spreadsheets (Excel/Google Sheets) for tracking campaign metrics
Video conferencing platforms (Zoom, Teams) for meetings
Document editing tools (Word, Google Docs) for proposal drafts

subtopics.subtopic_description: Familiarity with industry-standard technologies
notes: Proficient with major nonprofit CRMs, including Salesforce and Raiser’s Edge
Experienced in using Constant Contact and Mailchimp for donor outreach
Comfortable with major productivity and collaboration suites

subtopics.subtopic_description: Interoperability or integration issues between tools
notes: Occasionally faces challenges syncing CRM data with email marketing platforms
Manual data transfers required between spreadsheets and CRM
Desires better integration between donor database and reporting tools

subtopics.subtopic_description: Familiarity with fundamental AI/ML concepts and terminology
notes: Understands basic AI and machine learning concepts such as natural language processing
Aware of how LLMs generate text and analyze data
Familiar with AI-driven segmentation and audience targeting

subtopics.subtopic_description: Names of specific AI software/platforms currently used in work
notes: Uses OpenAI’s GPT-based tools for drafting donor communications
Employs AI-powered features in CRM systems for donor insights
Leverages AI tools for automating grant research and summarization

subtopics.subtopic_description: Frequency and purpose of AI tool application (specific use cases)
notes: Uses LLMs daily to brainstorm and draft emails, letters, and reports
Applies AI tools weekly for segmenting donor lists and analyzing giving trends
Utilizes AI for initial screening of grant opportunities several times per month

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific examples of AI success and failure experiences (lessons learned)
notes: AI-generated thank you letters have increased donor response rates
AI sometimes misinterprets nuanced donor relationships, requiring manual review
AI-based grant research significantly reduced time spent on opportunity identification
Over-reliance on AI can lead to less personalized donor communications

subtopics.subtopic_description: Availability of organizational training or peer resources for AI use
notes: Participated in internal training sessions on AI tools for fundraising
Shares best practices for AI use with colleagues during team meetings
Can access online tutorials and vendor-provided resources as needed

topic: AI Interaction Style and Workflow Change

subtopics.subtopic_description: Preferred mode of interaction: independent versus step-by-step collaboration
notes: Prefers starting independently with AI drafts, then customizes output
Occasionally collaborates with AI in real time for brainstorming sessions
Uses AI as a first-pass tool, followed by manual refinement

subtopics.subtopic_description: Style of human-AI teaming (e.g., advisor, assistant, co-worker)
notes: Views AI primarily as an efficient digital assistant
Sometimes treats AI as a brainstorming partner for campaign ideas
Relies on AI for support in research and drafting, but retains final decision control

subtopics.subtopic_description: Willingness and openness to adopting new AI-driven workflows
notes: Open to experimenting with new AI-driven features in core fundraising tools
Advocates for AI adoption if it increases efficiency or donor impact
Willing to pilot new AI workflows before recommending broader rollout

subtopics.subtopic_description: Preference for conversational vs. command-based interfaces (communication dynamics)
notes: Prefers conversational interfaces for drafting and editing text
Finds chat-based AI tools more intuitive for brainstorming
Uses command-based interfaces for data analysis or report generation

subtopics.subtopic_description: Extent to which tasks rely on specialized, tacit domain knowledge
notes: Major donor stewardship relies heavily on nuanced, relationship-based knowledge
Grant writing requires understanding of specific funder preferences and priorities
Some aspects of donor segmentation can be standardized, but personalization is key

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of trust in AI outputs for work tasks and critical decisions
notes: Trusts AI for drafting and data summarization, but always reviews output
Prefers to verify AI-generated content for sensitive donor communications
Reluctant to delegate final decision-making to AI for high-stakes proposals

subtopics.subtopic_description: Ideal balance of human effort and AI automation for specific tasks
notes: Prefers AI automation for repetitive or data-heavy tasks like list segmentation
Maintains human oversight for personalized outreach and strategic planning
Sees AI as a tool to augment, not replace, professional judgment

subtopics.subtopic_description: Conditions under which high automation is acceptable or threatening
notes: Accepts high automation for administrative data processing
Finds automation threatening if it risks losing donor relationship quality
Supports automation as long as manual review and customization remain possible

topic: AI Impact on Skills and Job Security

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived impact of AI on the importance of existing skills (enhanced vs. reduced)
notes: Believes AI enhances the value of strategic and relationship-building skills
Views writing and communication skills as even more important when refining AI drafts
Sees basic administrative skills as less critical due to automation

subtopics.subtopic_description: Emerging skills or new areas of responsibility created by AI
notes: Developed skills in prompt engineering for effective AI use
Takes responsibility for evaluating and selecting appropriate AI tools
Monitors AI output quality and ensures alignment with organizational voice

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of concern about AI replacing specific tasks or the overall role
notes: Low concern about AI replacing core donor relationship activities
Acknowledges some administrative tasks may be fully automated in future
Comfortable with evolving role focused on strategy and oversight

subtopics.subtopic_description: Availability of override mechanisms or manual checks for AI-driven processes
notes: Always reviews AI-generated donor communications before sending
Has final approval authority over automated grant application drafts
Regularly checks AI-segmented donor lists for errors or misclassification

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived change in team or company policies regarding AI adoption
notes: Organization has become more open to piloting AI tools in the last 2 years
Policies now encourage responsible AI use with human oversight
Regularly discusses AI-related best practices in team meetings

subtopics.subtopic_description: General outlook on AI's broader societal and industry impact
notes: Sees AI as a force multiplier for positive change in the nonprofit sector
Believes AI will make fundraising more efficient and accessible
Cautiously optimistic about AI’s role in reducing administrative burden

subtopics.subtopic_description: Personal beliefs about the ethics and risks of AI in the workplace
notes: Supports ethical guidelines for transparency and donor privacy in AI use
Wary of AI-generated content lacking authenticity or cultural sensitivity
Advocates for clear boundaries on automated decision-making

subtopics.subtopic_description: Missing AI tools or features that would be most beneficial in the future
notes: Desires smarter AI-driven donor segmentation and personalization tools
Wishes for AI that can automatically draft impact reports using real-time data
Seeks better integration between AI tools and existing CRMs

subtopics.subtopic_description: Predicted evolution of their job in the next 5-10 years with AI integration
notes: Anticipates spending more time on strategy and major donor engagement
Expects routine communications and reporting to be handled largely by AI
Believes new job functions will emerge around AI tool oversight and optimization

subtopics.subtopic_description: Concrete steps they would want their organization to take regarding AI strategy
notes: Implement regular training on AI best practices for fundraising staff
Establish clear guidelines for ethical AI use and content review
Invest in unified platforms that seamlessly integrate AI with core systems
Encourage pilot projects to evaluate new AI-driven fundraising solutions
